journal_entry.description: Customer deposit received for moving services to be performed in one month
journal_entry.rationale: Under IFRS 15, revenue is recognized when performance obligations are satisfied. Since the moving services will be performed in one month, the $5,000 deposit received represents a contract liability (unearned revenue/customer deposit), not revenue. The debit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly identifies an asset increase (Cash/Bank). The credit tuple (1,0,0,0,0,0) correctly identifies a liability increase (Unearned Revenue/Customer Deposits). No tax is applicable on this deposit as it is an advance payment; HST/GST will be recorded when services are performed and revenue is recognized.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit via bank transfer, Atlas debits its chequing account (bank account = asset increase). The deposit is for services to be performed in the future, so this is an advance payment that increases Atlas's cash asset.

debit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit via bank transfer, Atlas debits its chequing account (bank account = asset increase). The deposit is for services to be performed in the future, so this is an advance payment that increases Atlas's cash asset.

output.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, but we classify CREDITS only, so this is on the debit side. (2) The deposit creates an obligation to perform services = unearned revenue/deferred revenue, which is a liability increase. Therefore, the credit side has one line: liability increase for the unearned revenue.

credit_classifier.reason: Upon receiving the $5,000 deposit, Atlas has two credit-side effects: (1) Cash/chequing account increases = asset increase, but we classify CREDITS only, so this is on the debit side. (2) The deposit creates an obligation to perform services = unearned revenue/deferred revenue, which is a liability increase. Therefore, the credit side has one line: liability increase for the unearned revenue.

output.reason: Entry correctly records customer deposit as unearned revenue per IFRS 15. Debit to Cash ($5,000) reflects asset increase from bank transfer. Credit to Unearned Revenue ($5,000) reflects liability increase for obligation to perform future services. Entry balances (debits = credits). No tax applicable on advance payment - tax will be recorded when services are performed and revenue recognized. All accounts, amounts, and directionality are correct.

approver.reason: Entry correctly records customer deposit as unearned revenue per IFRS 15. Debit to Cash ($5,000) reflects asset increase from bank transfer.
